questions.question: Write the semaphore solution for Producer-Consumer with bounded buffer of size N.
questions.answer:
Semaphores: mutex=1 (buffer access), empty=N (empty slots), full=0 (filled slots).

Producer: wait(empty), wait(mutex), add_item(), signal(mutex), signal(full)
Consumer: wait(full), wait(mutex), remove_item(), signal(mutex), signal(empty)

Key: Order matters! For producer: empty before mutex. For consumer: full before mutex. This prevents deadlock.

questions.question: Explain why the naive dining philosophers solution causes deadlock and give a solution.
questions.answer:
Naive: Each philosopher picks left then right chopstick. If all pick left simultaneously, all wait for right forever → deadlock.

Solution (Asymmetric): Odd philosophers pick left first, even pick right first. Breaks circular wait. Example: P0 picks right(1), P1 picks left(1) - they compete for same chopstick, one succeeds, other waits, no circular chain.

questions.question: In the first readers-writers solution, why might writers starve?
questions.answer: First solution gives readers preference. As long as read_count > 0, writers wait. If new readers keep arriving before all current readers finish, write_lock is never released. Writers wait indefinitely while continuous stream of readers proceeds. Solution: Use writer preference or fair queuing.
